message.content: add a debounce to the search input — it's firing too many requests

content.text: I'll add a custom useDebounce hook in src/hooks/.

message.content: no — we already have one at @/hooks/useDebouncedValue. Don't write a parallel implementation. Always grep for existing utilities before adding new ones.

content.text: Got it — switching to the existing @/hooks/useDebouncedValue. (Earlier I assumed there was none because I jumped straight to implementation.) Let me grep first next time.

message.content: perfect. Also — recurring pattern in this codebase: any input that drives a fetch needs to be wrapped with useDebouncedValue *before* it lands in the effect's deps. Otherwise we get race conditions. Worth flagging in implement-feature skill.

content.text: Noted. The race condition pattern (fetch before debounce settles) keeps biting on this kind of search/filter input. Adding the wrap-debounce-before-deps rule to my mental checklist.

message.content: and one more thing for global memory: I prefer `rg --multiline` over default grep — it handles cross-line patterns the default mode misses. Use it as your default when searching this repo.

content.text: Will switch to rg --multiline as default. Done — the SearchBox now uses useDebouncedValue with a 300ms delay.
